description: Canonical structural graph exported from SQLite in the fixed 6-slot composite-key scheme: table + column + concept nodes, has_column edges (table -> column), and references edges (child column -> parent column) built from declared foreign keys, all with unified abbreviated unique_ids. Covers the business ERP tables (schema_* metadata tables excluded — the graph models the domain, not its own bookkeeping); the semantic ``resolves_to`` layer is active and node-guarded — each SME-curated elevation is an edge from a column node to the CONCEPT node it expresses under a business perspective (M2 re-point), carrying the binary weight gate and the raw priority_weight. Concept nodes are perspective-agnostic and carry the M3 payload (concept_type, domain, description as the definition, and the synonyms/tags arrays, emitted from schema_concepts); perspective is never stored on the node — it lives only on the resolves_to edge. M4 adds metrics: a metric concept carries a dialect-agnostic computation_template, and the resolves_to edges that bind its template {variable} placeholders to physical columns carry variable_name. A concept may exist without a resolves_to edge (an orphan glossary term) until a real ERP column is onboarded.

key_scheme.template: table|concept : column|entity : family : perspective : predicate|none : unique_id|none

reserved_tokens.entity: slot 2 (index 1): placeholder marking a TABLE or CONCEPT node (the entity itself — no column)
reserved_tokens.none: slots 5-6 (index 4-5): placeholder marking a NODE (no predicate / no unique_id)
reserved_tokens.system: slot 4 (index 3): reserved structural-layer scope (tables, columns, FK edges)
reserved_tokens.canonical: slot 4 (index 3): reserved perspective-agnostic scope for CONCEPT nodes (a concept spans all perspectives; the perspective attaches on the resolves_to edge, not the concept)

key_scheme.name_constraints: A real column may never be named 'entity' or 'none'; a real table may never be named 'none'; a business perspective may never be 'system' or 'canonical'; a concept may never be named any grammar token ('entity', 'none', 'system', 'canonical', 'structural', 'semantic'). The exporter hard-fails if any source name collides.
key_scheme.parse_by: fixed slot positions: NODE iff slot[4]=='none' and slot[5]=='none' (concept node if slot[2]=='semantic'; binding node if slot[2]=='manifest'; else table node if slot[1]=='entity'; else column node — both structural); otherwise EDGE, whose family is slot[2] (a manifest edge is binds_table).

rules.payload: M3: concept_type, domain, description (the definition), and authored JSON-array synonyms / tags. M4: a metric concept (concept_type=='metric') also carries a dialect-agnostic computation_template with named {variable} placeholders (NULL for non-metric concepts). The perspective stays OFF the node (it attaches on the resolves_to edge — dual-namespace). A concept may be a glossary-only node with no resolves_to edge yet.

rules.status: active (node-guarded; column -> concept node, M2 re-point; uid is concept-stable)
rules.payload: M4: an edge that binds a column to a metric concept also carries variable_name — the template {variable} this column fills (NULL for categorical / measure elevations).

v21 living-manifest binding: an SME-approved snippet identity. Carries binding_key, concept_anchor, logic_type, its business perspective, and the sme_justification as description. Its structural fingerprint (base-table set) is expressed as binds_table edges, not stored on the node. Only APPROVED snippets become binding nodes.

rules.status: active (one edge per base table in the snippet's signed structural fingerprint)

unique_id_grammar.structural: structural edges (has_column, references) share one COUNTED slot-5 grammar
unique_id_grammar.abbrev: first 3 alphanumeric chars of each part, uppercased; collisions are expected and resolved by the uniqifier
unique_id_grammar.uniqifier: structural only: allocated (not derived) per (perspective, edge_type, table, column|entity) prefix; default '001'
unique_id_grammar.edge_type_key_scope: one edge_type key per perspective — the 3-char edge_type abbreviation is namespaced within its perspective, not global
unique_id_grammar.resolves_to_uid: the resolves_to uid is concept-STABLE and DERIVED (not counted): <perspective>_RES_<table>_<column>_<8-hex SHA1 of perspective|table|column|concept|field_component>; deriving it from the edge's natural key means adding/removing one meaning never renumbers a column's other resolves_to edges (M2 invariant)

nodes.description: Employee master records
Supplier certification records (CoC, FAI, PPAP, 8130-3, Material Test Report)
Customer order header — demand-side sales orders with site and status
Customer order line items — part, quantity, unit price per order
GL economic-event log — one row per posted costing event (material issue, labor, burden, job completion) with source-document provenance
Finished-goods inventory ledger lines — signed dollar movements into (+) and out of (-) the FG account, hung off gl_events
Per-job cost-element detail lines (material / labor / burden) tying each posted dollar back to its work order
Raw-materials inventory ledger lines — signed dollar movements into (+) and out of (-) the RM account, hung off gl_events
Work-in-process inventory ledger lines — signed dollar movements into (+) and out of (-) the WIP account, hung off gl_events
Inventory transaction distribution — links an IN movement to an OUT movement (lot genealogy)
Inventory movement ledger (CLASS R/A/I x TYPE I/O) — receipts, issues, adjustments
Labor time postings against work order operations (clock-in/out, hours, cost)
Raw material issues from stock to WIP work orders (quantity, unit cost, total cost)
Work order routing steps — sequence, resource, estimated vs actual hours and costs
Part / item master — description, class, UOM, cost, revision, material spec, CAGE code
On-hand quantity of a part at a warehouse location — physical inventory snapshot for reconciliation
AP payable line detail extending payables — part, PO, quantity, amount
Accounts-payable headers (acts as the invoice header) linked to purchase orders — three-way match and payment status
Purchase order line items (part, quantity, unit cost, line total)
Purchase order headers for material and outside-service buys
Accounts-receivable headers (customer invoice header) linked to customer orders — billing and payment status
AR receivable line detail extending receivable — part, order line, quantity, amount
AR installment payment records — one row per installment per invoice, linked to the cash-receipt GL event
Goods receipts against purchase orders — quantity ordered vs received, inspection status
Line items on a goods receipt — one row per part received, linked to the PO line for line-level three-way match

Resource requirements (labor / material / burden) per routing operation; component_type PART = as-designed routing standard (engineering, per-unit) vs WORK_ORDER = as-built actuals (manufacturing, batch)
Outside service definitions (anodize, heat treat, NDT, plating, painting)
Shop work centers and outside-service buckets (machine, labor, service types)
Site dimension — physical plant / location anchoring inventory, orders, and work orders
Supplier master — name, category, certification level, payment terms, lead time
Traced lot / serial record of a part — production and expiration dates, quantities
Bridge linking a trace lot to an inventory transaction (qty-weighted) for genealogy
Physical stocking location (warehouse) belonging to a site — anchors part on-hand
Work order master — part, quantity, status, routing template, accumulated actual costs
Quantity of on-hand stock already committed / reserved to existing orders
Uncommitted inventory available to promise to new customer orders (on hand minus allocated)
Validity state of a certification (active / expired / revoked / pending)
Kind of certification held (material / process / quality-system / etc.)
Defect severity from cost impact perspective - focuses on financial exposure
Defect severity from customer visibility perspective - focuses on brand risk
Defect severity from quality control perspective - focuses on product conformance

nodes.computation_template: AVG(CASE WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} <= {required_date} THEN 1.0 WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} > {required_date} THEN 0.0 ELSE NULL END)
nodes.description: Delivery metrics for cost/penalty calculation

nodes.computation_template: AVG(CASE WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} <= {required_date} THEN 1.0 WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} > {required_date} THEN 0.0 ELSE NULL END)
nodes.description: Delivery metrics for operational planning

nodes.computation_template: AVG(CASE WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} <= {required_date} THEN 1.0 WHEN {receipt_date} IS NOT NULL AND {receipt_date} > {required_date} THEN 0.0 ELSE NULL END)
nodes.description: Delivery metrics for supplier scorecard
Order quantity that minimizes combined ordering and holding cost (classic EOQ formula)
Equipment status for regulatory compliance
Equipment status for maintenance planning
Equipment status for production scheduling
Failure severity based on repair/replacement cost
Failure severity based on production impact
Failure severity based on safety implications
Finished goods produced over a period: FG_COMPLETION inflow into the Finished Goods bucket per part and job
Signed running balance of a perpetual inventory bucket (Raw Materials / WIP / Finished Goods) from the gl_* sub-ledgers; the approved ledger_inventorybalance snippet is the authoritative derivation
Cost element of a job-cost ledger line (labor / material / burden / service) rolled up per work order in gl_job_cost_detail
Elapsed time from placing a replenishment order to receiving the stock (in days)
Expected demand for a part over its replenishment lead time (average daily demand x lead time)
Kind of ledger posting event in the gl_events audit trail (RM_ISSUE / LABOR / BURDEN / FG_COMPLETION)
Material issued to jobs over a period: RM_ISSUE outflow from the Raw Materials bucket, reported as positive issued value per part and job
Highest stock level a part is replenished up to (max in a min/max policy)
Lowest stock level allowed before replenishment is required (min in a min/max policy)
NCM disposition from cost recovery standpoint
NCM disposition from quality standpoint
OEE for shift/line performance tracking
OEE for capital investment decisions
Physical quantity of a part currently in stock (book inventory on hand)
Execution state of a routing operation (queued -> started -> complete)
Order status from revenue recognition perspective
Order status as visible to customer
Order status representing lifecycle stage in fulfillment
How a part is sourced: engineered/made vs bought vs raw vs hardware vs outside service (engineering make/buy intent)
Lifecycle state of a purchase order (open -> approved -> received -> closed)
Quantity read through the engineering lens: a per-unit, as-designed requirement basis (engineering normalizes material needs to a single unit)
Quantity read through the manufacturing lens: a production batch / lot size to build or fulfill (qty > 1)
Inspection disposition of a received shipment (pending -> pass / fail)
Inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered (lead-time demand + safety stock)
Requirement read through the engineering lens: an as-designed routing standard on a PART component (per-unit basis); component_type='PART'
Requirement read through the manufacturing lens: as-built actuals on a WORK_ORDER component (batch basis); component_type='WORK_ORDER'
Buffer inventory held to absorb demand and lead-time variability and prevent stockouts
Direction of an inventory transaction: stock receipt (in) vs issue (out)
PO/receipt/invoice three-way match status used to validate AP invoices
Warehouse/site a stock move is distributed to (inventory traced by move)
Work order lifecycle stage on the shop floor (Open->Released->In Process->Complete->Closed)
Allocated quantity per part: sum of open customer-order-line quantities committed against on-hand stock. Dataset derivation over customer_order_line — the approved snippet is the definition.
Available-to-promise quantity per part: on-hand stock minus total open customer-order commitments. Dataset derivation — no single column owns ATP; the approved snippet is the definition.

EOQ proxy: average observed PO order quantity (po_line.quantity) reflects the historically settled replenishment lot size. Without D/S/H cost parameters in this schema the empirical PO quantity is the closest available proxy. Column anchor: po_line.quantity. Falls back to reorder_point when no PO history exists.
Lead time summary by part class (avg/min/max days, long-lead count) — MRP planning-horizon decision support query against the part table

Lead-time demand: average daily demand (customer_order_line) × part.lead_time_days. Column anchor: part.lead_time_days is the planning window factor. Dataset derivation — approved snippet is the authoritative definition.

Maximum stock quantity: reorder_point + average historical PO replenishment qty (po_line.quantity aggregated per part). Column anchor: po_line.quantity captures the observed replenishment lot size. Dataset derivation — approved snippet is the authoritative definition.
Minimum stock quantity: part.reorder_point is the min level in a min/max replenishment policy. Direct column read with below/at/above status classification.
On-hand stock levels by part class with inventory value and reorder-point flag counts — primary stock-status query against the part table
Parts at or below reorder point — replenishment trigger report against the part table reorder_point threshold; primary MRP replenishment planning query

Safety stock proxy: reorder point minus estimated lead-time demand (avg daily demand from customer_order_line × lead_time_days). Column anchor: part.reorder_point encodes the safety buffer in this ERP (ROP = SS + LT demand). Dataset derivation — approved snippet is the authoritative definition.
Chronological audit trace of gl_events postings for a job, carrying the originating source document (table + row key); optional :job_id filter and :start_date/:end_date horizon guards.
Finished goods produced over a period: FG_COMPLETION postings (signed positive into the FG bucket) per part and job; :start_date/:end_date horizon guards.
Signed running balance per perpetual inventory bucket (RM/WIP/FG) from the gl_* sub-ledgers; optional :as_of_date horizon guard. Single UNION ALL over the three bucket tables, no joins.
Job cost roll-up by work order and cost element (LABOR/MATERIAL/BURDEN/SERVICE) from gl_job_cost_detail; optional :job_id filter guard and :start_date/:end_date horizon guards.
Material issued over a period: RM_ISSUE postings (signed negative out of the RM bucket) reported as positive issued value per part and job; :start_date/:end_date horizon guards.
Operations delivery performance: inbound on-time receipt rate per PO compared to required date — flags overdue open POs for expediting and tracks material delivery reliability
Compliance view: cert-required receipts with inspection and certificate status joined to shop resources — verifies cert-required material processed on each resource has a current certificate
Maintenance scheduling view: open-operation count, remaining run hours, and last close date per active shop resource — identifies resources due for PM or blocked by open operations

Production scheduling view: active shop resources with their current open work order operations, scheduled windows, and estimated cost — used by supervisors for machine loading and scheduling decisions
OEE operational proxy: actual vs. scheduled run hours per active shop resource, grouped by resource for shift-level line efficiency and throughput reporting
OEE strategic proxy: cost efficiency and utilization aggregated by resource type for capital investment, capacity planning, and equipment replacement decisions
Per-work-order cost breakdown (labor, burden, outside service, material) for WIP valuation and job costing — joins against live work_order table
Work order status summary grouped by lifecycle state with quantity and cost rollup — primary production planning query against live work_order table
Open PO spend aggregated by supplier with count and total value — payables aging and supplier spend analysis joining purchase_order to suppliers
Purchase orders fully received — every po_line has receipt coverage >= ordered quantity, computed line-by-line via receiving_line.po_line_id; core receiving-completion procurement view
Purchase orders unreceived or short — at least one po_line not fully received, with unreceived vs short line counts and outstanding quantity; core expediting / open-order procurement view

Partial-receipt accrual exposure (PO-line accrual): PO lines with received qty > 0 but < ordered qty whose received portion is not fully voucher-covered — open PRA exposure per the TWM accrual guides (docs/my-mrp-kb/07-three-way-match)
Purchase order status list with supplier name, type, dates, and value — core AP dashboard query joining purchase_order to suppliers

Consolidated three-way-match coverage spine at receipt/voucher row grain: flat base-table joins only (no CTEs/derived tables), po_line->purchase_order INNER then all LEFT, covering Not Received / Received-Uninvoiced / Partially Invoiced / Matched / Over-Invoiced states; sibling exception views become filters over this spine

Uninvoiced receipts exception report (three-way match): receipts with at least one receiving line whose non-cancelled payable coverage is below the received quantity or absent entirely — refactored from the private-repo ground truth '203 3WM Uninvoiced Receipts'

AR aging report (Open + Disputed invoices only) bucketed by days past due_date — Current / 1-30 / 31-60 / >60 days — relative to a data-derived as-of date (MAX payment_date from Paid invoices). After collect_june2026_ar, all pre-July-2026 invoices are Paid and return zero rows here, confirming the cash-to-cash cycle closed correctly.
Customer-facing work order delivery status: classifies each order as On Time, Late, Overdue, At Risk, or On Schedule against required delivery dates
Outside-service supplier list (NADCAP-certified vendors for heat treat, NDT, anodize, plating) — used when routing operations to outside vendors
Active supplier list with category, certification level, lead time, and performance rating — core supplier scorecard query against live suppliers table
